relative_path: Protocols, instructions/Tissue dissociation, organoids and iPDCs/Tissue_ processing_inventory keeping_NKL_ONCOSYS.docx
full_text:
Protocol for HGSC tissue, and ascites collection and processing

Start date:

Preparation prior to sample collection

Bring liquid nitrogen (LN2) - Färkkilä metal container at lab bench, fill up approximately ¾

Go to 5th floor if there is no LN left at 2nd floor (do not use elevator when carrying LN)

Use protective equipment and mark 1L in log when LN is securely in container

Allow LN to evaporate some time before closing the lid to avoid buildup of pressure

Inside the fume hood, prepare 4 % formaldehyde solution (fixative) in a small plastic jar with ratio of 5 mL 37-41% formaldehyde + 45 mL 1x PBS (non-sterile) --> add a total of 10 mL of formaldehyde + 90 mL of 1xPBS and mark jar with date, content and initials.

Bring ice and switch on the +370C incubator (located in Färkkilä lab below the lab bench).

Place 4% formaldehyde on ice and prepare the rest of basket:

Mark 6x Eppendorfs with sample number, tube information (2x DNA, 2x RNA, 2x protein), date, initials

Mark 2x histocassettes with sample number + initials + date with xylene resistant pen

Two Merkasoft sheets

Extra Eppendorfs + histocassettes

Pens, shoe covers, gloves

New sterile HBSS, surgical knives etc if needed

Keep the lab phone and keys (both found at Anastasiya’s desk draw) with you all the time when Anastasiya is not around.

Protocol:

Sample ID: SXXX_p

Dissection at Näytehuone NKL

Have the basket + LN ready for when they call from NKL that the sample can be collected. Go to the sample collection room swiftly once they call and remember to ask about whether the diagnosis has been confirmed (kindly ask them to call you once it's confirmed if diagnosis is not known yet).

Place a MERKASOFT paper inside the hood (soft side up). Place the tissue onto a 10 cm plate (found at NKL); if the sample is too big transfer it to a 15 cm plate. Take pictures with a ruler using the lab phone. Select the tumor region / nodule for snap freezing, formalin fixation and tissue processing. Preferably all from the same nodule. Trim away the surrounding soft tissue, membrane and largest blood vessels. Cut the tumor into 6 small pieces for snap freezing into LN2 🡪 2 piece for DNA, RNA, and protein isolation, freeze 1 piece per tube. Dissect adjacent tissue piece for formalin fixation, big enough for analysis but small enough in thickness to fit into the cassette. Place the histocasette (with the tissue) into the 4% formaldehyde on ice.

If there is a normal-looking omentum area, remove macroscopically visible tumor nodules and transfer to a 50 mL falcon. Weigh the tissue piece, place it on ice and bring it to the lab for later transfer to -800C. Transfer remaining tumor tissue into a jar with HBSS for cell culture, keep it on ice and bring it to the lab.

Mark down the time between tumor sample excision and snap freezing of samples (should not exceed 20min), also take a picture of ascites tube if this was given and place it on ice. Remove all biobank stickers from tubes and label them with sample ID – keep stickers safe and hidden until placing them into the locked PI office cabinet (key to cabinet also in anastasiya’s desk draw).

Cleaning once sample is secured: Transfer all sample waste to biohazard bin, place tweezers on plastic tray, place scalpel in collection container, clean fume hood and ruler with the disinfectant in hood and hand towel, then discard gloves to biohazard bin and wash hands thoroughly.

Transfer the remaining tumor tissue into a HBSS jar (the same jar in which they keep the tissue), and keep the jar and ascites containing falcon on ice. Bring them to the lab along with LN2 container.

At ONCOSYS Lab

Give the HBSS jar containing the tumor tissue to ONCOSYS cell culture for tissue processing.

Transfer the snap frozen tissue pieces to -80. Note down the location, and the box number. Later update in the G-drive inventory.

Place the biobank sticker in a folder inside the Väharautio’s office cupboard (take the keys from Saun’s desk), Write sample ID, tissue site, date, and your initials on the sticker.

Update Benchling, G-drive sample table, and wiki sample table.

Transfer the pictures of the tissues taken at Näytehuone NKL to Benchling, and also to L-drive.

If ascites were given, keep on ice and process it:

Pipette 2x 1,5ml of ascites into 2x Safe-lock 1,5 ml Eppendorfs (marked pellet 1 and pellet 2)

Centrifuge at Vähärautio lab for 10min at +4C at 1500rpm

Transfer supernatants into 2x Safe-lock 1,5 ml Eppendorfs (marked supernatant 1 and supernatant 2)

Snap freeze all 4 Eppendorfs in LN, then transfer to –80C freezer to ascites box. Update the location and box number to the G-drive inventory

(Transfer left over ascites in 50ml falcon to –80C green rack if Meri lab cannot collect ascites sample from +4C färkkilä lab fridge)
